top_signals.title: Oil Prices Rise as Iran Talks Stall and Inventories Shrink
top_signals.summary: Oil prices edged higher on Thursday, as investors monitored peace talks between the United States and Iran, while supply tightness and U.S. inventory drawdowns provided some support.Brent crude futures LCOc1 rose 40 cents, or 0.4%, to $105.42 a barrel by 0809 GMT, and U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Malians tell of torture and killings by army and Russian fighters
top_signals.summary: According to data from conflict monitor ACLED, Malian government operations since 2020 have caused more than 8,500 deaths, about half of them civilians.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Russia launches unannounced nuclear exercise, including Belarusian launch sites
top_signals.summary: The dual messaging − nuclear muscle-flexing at home, high-profile diplomacy abroad − fits a pattern the Kremlin has used repeatedly.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
